theme: The floor knows what time it is, what's shipping, and that things are on fire — make it show
visual_observations: Desktop 1280x800, Crushing It vibe active. Bloberto sits at his manager desk top-center with 'CVO' nameplate and purple glow ring. Three desks top row: Maya (palette), Sofia (magnifier), Carlos — each with tiny glowing desk-lamp SVG (which at a glance reads as a trophy, but it is not). Three desks bottom row with different worker assignments. SHIP IT text appears on the left-wall whiteboard rectangle (pure text on a white div — no animation, no connection to workers). Conference table center-lower: a large orbit ring of chairs with the idle-wander animation circling it — striking, but looks like a loading spinner rather than a meeting. Coffee corner top-right shows steam wisps (implemented, subtle but effective). Window right-side is a small dark rectangle — at 1280px wide it barely reads as a window. No wall clock anywhere on the floor. No task context visible on desks without hovering. No trophies or achievement count anywhere on the floor. Mobile view: office floor crammed into ~300px width, desk labels tiny, lots of blank purple space below the office.

ideas.title: Office Wall Clock
ideas.description: There is zero time orientation on the office floor. The window widget shows time-of-day sky gradients but you cannot distinguish 9am from 2pm at a glance. An analog wall clock anchors the entire space in real time and costs 20 lines. Implementation: add a WallClock component — SVG ~22px wide, positioned left:22%, top:2.5% (between the office sign and whiteboard). Clock face: <circle r=9 fill=var(--surface2) stroke=var(--border)/>, hour hand <line y2=-4.5 stroke=var(--text) strokeWidth=1.5 transform=rotate({hourDeg})/>, minute hand <line y2=-6.5 stroke=var(--accent) strokeWidth=1 transform=rotate({minuteDeg})/>, center dot. Update every 60s using useState(new Date()) hook. hourDeg = (h%12)*30 + m*0.5, minuteDeg = m*6. During after-hours: face fill=#1e1b4b, accent indigo. During crushing: minute hand gets @keyframes tick-fast { 0%,49%{transform:rotate(0)} 50%,100%{transform:rotate(6deg)} } 0.5s steps(1) infinite — a frantic visual tick. During slow-day: minute-hand transition is sluggish 2s ease. WHY: Every real office has a clock. Without one, Bloberto's HQ feels like a dream space untethered from reality. A wall clock is the smallest possible element that makes the floor feel like a real place where real time passes.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Team Progress Thermometer
ideas.description: The whiteboard (left wall) shows one vibe quote: SHIP IT, FLOW, HELP. Great one-liners that waste 80% of the surface. The whiteboard is the largest labeled prop on the floor and currently communicates one word. Directly below the vibe text, add a team progress bar: const avgProgress = Math.round(workingWorkers.reduce((s,w) => s + (w.progress||0), 0) / Math.max(workingWorkers.length, 1)). Render a .wb-progress-track div (width:78%, height:3px, margin:3px auto 0, background:rgba(255,255,255,0.1), border-radius:2px) containing a .wb-progress-fill (width: avgProgress+'%', background: vibeAccentColor, transition: width 1.2s ease). Add a .wb-progress-pct span (font-size:0.38rem, color:rgba(255,255,255,0.5)) showing the number. When avgProgress hits 100: vibe text flickers to 'DONE!' for 3s, bar pulses: @keyframes wb-complete { 0%,100%{opacity:1} 50%{opacity:0.3; background:#22c55e} } over 0.6s 3 iterations. If no working workers, bar opacity:0. WHY: The whiteboard is prime real estate — the team's shared scoreboard. Converting it from a one-word vibe label into a live progress thermometer makes it the heartbeat of the floor. Someone glancing at the office tab instantly knows the team is at 43% through the sprint queue without clicking into the dashboard.

ideas.title: Trophy Shelf — Completion Memory Wall
ideas.description: The office floor has no permanent record of work done. Confetti fires for 2.5s and vanishes. 'Shipped it!' toasts last 3s and vanish. By end of a hard day the floor looks identical to an empty day — zero visual residue of effort. In the top-right corner (between the cactus at left:92% and the window), add .trophy-shelf: a horizontal SVG line (width:12%, top:18%, right:3%) with up to 5 .trophy-item spans (trophy emoji, font-size:0.7rem, evenly spaced). Count = activityEntries.filter(e => e.type === 'complete').length, capped at 5 visible with a +N counter badge if over 5. When a NEW completion is detected via useEffect (prevCount < currentCount), the newest trophy animates in: @keyframes trophy-drop { from { transform:translateY(-16px) scale(0.5); opacity:0; } to { transform:translateY(0) scale(1); opacity:1; } } over 0.4s spring, then a scale pulse 1->1.5->1 over 0.35s. WHY: This is the floor's missing achievement mechanic. Review 33 proposed trophy burst animations but the trophies themselves were never built — there is no trophy data anywhere in the codebase. A shelf that fills with trophies throughout the day transforms the wall from decor into a scoreboard. At the end of a crushing day, 5 trophies on the shelf tell the whole story.

ideas.title: Desk Task-Category Color Chips
ideas.description: Six desks, six workers, zero task context visible without hovering. The dashboard's task tag system (Bug / Ship / Design / QA / Data) is completely invisible in the office view. The office tells you 'people are working' but not WHAT is happening. Add a .desk__task-chip beneath each active working worker's nameplate: a div (height:8px, border-radius:3px, padding:0 3px, font-size:0.35rem, font-weight:700) showing the first tag from getTaskTags(worker.task). Bug -> background:rgba(239,68,68,0.2) color:#f87171. Ship -> rgba(249,115,22,0.2) color:#fb923c. Design -> rgba(168,85,247,0.2) color:#c084fc. QA -> rgba(59,130,246,0.2) color:#60a5fa. Data -> rgba(234,179,8,0.2) color:#facc15. No chip if untagged. The chip is small enough that the desk does not feel cluttered at 900px max-width. On hover, chip scales 1.4x alongside the existing hover card. WHY: This single change converts 'six people working' into a live team composition map. If you see red/red/orange/purple chips across four desks you immediately know there are two bugs in flight, a deploy, and a design sprint — without reading a single word.

ideas.title: Conference Table Standup State
ideas.description: The conference table has vibe-reactive chairs and glows with team energy — but has NO human presence annotation. Idle workers wander the lower floor independently, passing by the table without interacting with it. When idleWorkers.length >= 2, activate a hasMeeting boolean that triggers conf-table__standup state. Inside the ConferenceTable SVG, add a centered text element: <text x=52 y=56 textAnchor=middle fontSize=6 fill=rgba(255,255,255,0.35)>standup</text>. Animate: @keyframes standup-pulse { 0%,100%{opacity:0.3} 50%{opacity:0.7} } over 2s ease infinite. When hasMeeting is true, also bump chair circle strokeWidth from 0 to 0.5 and lighten fill by 15% via CSS transition. During after-hours, never show standup — replace with zzz text at opacity:0.15. WHY: The round table with orbit chairs is the best spatial storytelling element on the floor, but it's wasted as pure decor. When half the team is idle they're probably chatting or in standup. The label makes the space feel intentional and used rather than a decorative centerpiece.

ideas.title: Deploy Pipeline Ticker Strip
ideas.description: When any working worker has a Ship-tagged task (deploy/release/ci/pipeline — already detected by getTaskTags), there is no ambient signal that a deploy is in flight. Deploys are the highest-stakes moments in a dev team's day and right now they are invisible unless you hover a desk or check the dashboard. Add a .deploy-ticker strip: position:absolute; bottom:1.5%; left:8%; width:84%; height:14px; overflow:hidden; border-radius:4px; background:rgba(249,115,22,0.08); border:1px solid rgba(249,115,22,0.2); display:none — shown only when a Ship-tagged working worker exists. Inside: a scrolling .ticker-inner div containing duplicated text: 'deploying... building... running tests... pushing to prod...' repeated twice for seamless loop. Animation: @keyframes ticker-scroll { from{transform:translateX(0)} to{transform:translateX(-50%)} } 12s linear infinite. Font: 0.38rem, color:#fb923c, letter-spacing:0.08em. When that worker's status is error: strip flips to red (#ef4444), text changes to 'deploy failed... rolling back...'. Strip disappears when worker returns to idle (deploy complete). WHY: 'Dave is deploying to production right now' is not the same as 'Dave is writing code.' A 14px strip at the floor bottom creates the perfect ambient tension — users will glance at it nervously, exactly like watching a CI pipeline. Its absence when done is its own signal.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Panic Lap
ideas.description: When vibe transitions TO 'on-fire', Bloberto changes his face expression — but stays frozen at his manager desk. The on-fire state is an incident. The most human managerial response is physically running to check on everyone. Implementation: alongside VibeShockwave, detect prevVibe !== 'on-fire' && newVibe === 'on-fire' in a usePrevious comparison. Set isPanicking:true for 2200ms. When panicking, Bloberto's Character applies class char--panic-lap which overrides his position with: @keyframes panic-lap { 0%,100%{left:46%;top:4%} 18%{left:10%;top:30%} 36%{left:34%;top:30%} 54%{left:58%;top:30%} 72%{left:10%;top:52%} 90%{left:34%;top:52%} } over 2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1). Simultaneously, each desk that Bloberto 'visits' (tracked via a panicDeskIds Set updated in parallel) flashes border-color:#ef4444 for 0.3s. Bloberto's speech bubble fires 'ON IT!!' as the animation starts, disappears after 0.8s. State resets after 2200ms. WHY: A vibe change to on-fire is currently a color shift plus a shockwave ring. But the shockwave fires FROM the manager desk — it should MOVE the manager. One 2-second animation where the boss physically sprints desk-to-desk is the single most emotionally resonant moment this floor can produce. It turns an abstract state change into a narrative event anyone watching will remember and screenshot.
